exampleSentences: Anh ấy nấu cơm rất ngon.
Chị ấy mua áo rất đẹp.
Em uống nước này rất nhanh.
exampleSentencesTranslated: I drink this water very fast.
notes:
Rất means very. It makes an adjective or verb stronger.

It usually comes before adjectives or descriptive verbs: rất + adj/verb to boost intensity.  

It’s stronger than hơi (a bit) but more neutral than quá (too/so much).  
You don’t use it before nouns directly — it needs something to describe.

definitions.vi: Từ dùng để nhấn mạnh mức độ cao của tính chất hoặc trạng thái.
definitions.en: A word used to emphasize a high degree of quality or state; very.

notes_md:
**Rất** means **very**. It makes an adjective or verb stronger.

It usually comes **before adjectives or descriptive verbs**: *rất + adj/verb* to boost intensity.  

It’s stronger than **hơi** (a bit) but more neutral than **quá** (too/so much).  
You don’t use it before nouns directly — it needs something to describe.
sentenceBreakdowns: Anh ấy (he) + nấu (cooks) + cơm (rice) + rất (very) + ngon (delicious)
Chị ấy (she) + mua (buys) + áo (clothes) + rất (very) + đẹp (beautiful)
Em (I) + uống (drink) + nước này (this water) + rất (very) + nhanh (fast)

translation.en: She really likes to read books.
